---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 8a32a95c24be180511c0c46d8ef66f9b7b83fcf3
findings:
  - { id: judged-todo-free-only-frontmatter-positions, kind: judged, text: "ac-3 promises the created story lands 'TODO-free in every position whose field was actually filled,' and the outcome frames this as 'TODO-free wherever the author actually answered the form' against a problem statement complaining that scaffolds 'carry generic TODO placeholders.' But the field contract can only bind {{.Placeholder}} positions, and the embedded canonical story.md binds Problem/Outcome ONLY in frontmatter (problem:/outcome:). Its body sections '## Problem', '## Outcome', and '## Ac 1' are hardcoded literal prose 'TODO: design notes.' with no field binding, so a story created through the form on a default store (no template override) still lands carrying the literal string 'TODO: design notes.' under the Problem and Outcome headings even though the author answered those fields. The literal AC wording is technically satisfied (frontmatter is the only field-bound position), but the intent — answering Problem removes the Problem TODO a reader sees — is only half discharged for the common case. Notably the ac-3 e2e proof (e2e/tests/48) runs against a vocab-fixture OVERRIDE template whose body ('## Delivery Notes', no design-notes TODO) sidesteps this, and its assertions only check the frontmatter default placeholders ('TODO: replace with the real problem/outcome') are absent — so the default embedded-template behavior of body TODOs persisting beside filled fields is never exercised in a browser. (confidence 0.35)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Same cross-surface constraint as cli-creation's judged-todo-free-rendering-body-prose (already accepted-deviation): the form fills the frontmatter field-bound positions (the canonical source of truth), but the scaffold templates' BODY sections (## Problem/## Outcome carrying 'TODO: design notes.') are frozen byte-for-byte by spec/scaffold-templates and cannot be rewritten from this story. ac-3's literal wording ('TODO-free in every position whose field was actually filled') is technically satisfied — frontmatter is the only field-bound position, which the judge acknowledges — and fail-closed behavior is correct. The intent-gap (a reader still sees a body TODO under the Problem heading) is a cross-surface scaffold-body-TODO limitation, queued for ratification/stage-3 precision (either a shared 'field-bound positions' wording clarification across the creation-surface ACs, or a future scaffold-templates story binding body sections). Honesty slice, not a fix-class defect." }
not-resurfaced:
  - { id: judged-create-owners-value-shape, kind: judged, text: "Residual after the 0ad2879 fix: normalizeOwners closes the common paths (comma lists, bracketed literals, zero-name refusal by name), but an Owners value carrying a YAML-hostile character still reaches the strict-decode internal error the adjudicated finding said should never surface. An unbalanced bracket ('[alice') is not stripped and renders as the malformed literal '[[alice]'; a name containing a double quote or ': ' (e.g. 'O\"Brien' or 'role: alice') normalizes to a bracketed literal that safeScalar %q-quotes into a YAML STRING, so the owners: []string position fails DecodeSpec and the caller sees 'create scaffold failed self-validation: ...' rather than the named Owners-shape refusal. Fail-closed holds (nothing lands, TestBoardSpec_Create_OwnersNormalization pins the fixed paths), but the named-refusal guarantee is complete only for well-formed names — a narrow sliver of the original issue, disclosed rather than silently considered closed. (confidence 0.20)", disposition: accepted-deviation, note: "Disclosed residual: the named-refusal guarantee is complete for well-formed names; YAML-hostile names (unbalanced brackets, quotes, colons) fail closed with the generic self-validation error and nothing invalid ever lands. Completing the guarantee would require inventing an owner-name charset contract — a model-level question this story does not silently decide (provenance discipline); queued as a candidate model question, not a defect." }
digest: sha256:daa4f4df483b7c2de2df2abbc8f24b56c33e6bfef84daa994d3d003365b9a705
integrity: sha256:f5c20fd55c763e5e706d27388dd3eb966f77f6a0235543d07e4c5665a7c9eea4
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "I've reviewed the implementation against all four acceptance criteria. The implementation is largely faithful: `Fields` enumeration matches the pinned D-1 sets and mirrors Render's struct contract exactly (fieldKinds ≡ ScaffoldData fields); `actionCreate` shares stub-instantiate's guards, self-validates before plumbing, and binds implements edges to validated wall ACs; `commitdesign` resolves the feature override then falls back to the byte-pinned canonical. One semantic observation survives that the graph diff and computed findings miss.\n\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"todo-free-only-frontmatter-positions\",\"text\":\"ac-3 promises the created story lands 'TODO-free in every position whose field was actually filled,' and the outcome frames this as 'TODO-free wherever the author actually answered the form' against a problem statement complaining that scaffolds 'carry generic TODO placeholders.' But the field contract can only bind {{.Placeholder}} positions, and the embedded canonical story.md binds Problem/Outcome ONLY in frontmatter (problem:/outcome:). Its body sections '## Problem', '## Outcome', and '## Ac 1' are hardcoded literal prose 'TODO: design notes.' with no field binding, so a story created through the form on a default store (no template override) still lands carrying the literal string 'TODO: design notes.' under the Problem and Outcome headings even though the author answered those fields. The literal AC wording is technically satisfied (frontmatter is the only field-bound position), but the intent — answering Problem removes the Problem TODO a reader sees — is only half discharged for the common case. Notably the ac-3 e2e proof (e2e/tests/48) runs against a vocab-fixture OVERRIDE template whose body ('## Delivery Notes', no design-notes TODO) sidesteps this, and its assertions only check the frontmatter default placeholders ('TODO: replace with the real problem/outcome') are absent — so the default embedded-template behavior of body TODOs persisting beside filled fields is never exercised in a browser.\",\"confidence\":0.35}]}" }
frozen: { at: 2026-07-22, commit: 8a32a95c24be180511c0c46d8ef66f9b7b83fcf3 }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/creation-form@8a32a95c24be180511c0c46d8ef66f9b7b83fcf3, spec/creation-form@04e6c0ff27a3644516b3aad6667c108c7c1af1b4], digest: sha256:daa4f4df483b7c2de2df2abbc8f24b56c33e6bfef84daa994d3d003365b9a705, integrity: sha256:f5c20fd55c763e5e706d27388dd3eb966f77f6a0235543d07e4c5665a7c9eea4, model: sha256:a8b2e3c1eb4defba461355077a2b82ae13db212beaad6e78eab05db51e5b6721 }
---
# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-todo-free-only-frontmatter-positions** [accepted-deviation]: ac-3 promises the created story lands 'TODO-free in every position whose field was actually filled,' and the outcome frames this as 'TODO-free wherever the author actually answered the form' against a problem statement complaining that scaffolds 'carry generic TODO placeholders.' But the field contract can only bind {{.Placeholder}} positions, and the embedded canonical story.md binds Problem/Outcome ONLY in frontmatter (problem:/outcome:). Its body sections '## Problem', '## Outcome', and '## Ac 1' are hardcoded literal prose 'TODO: design notes.' with no field binding, so a story created through the form on a default store (no template override) still lands carrying the literal string 'TODO: design notes.' under the Problem and Outcome headings even though the author answered those fields. The literal AC wording is technically satisfied (frontmatter is the only field-bound position), but the intent — answering Problem removes the Problem TODO a reader sees — is only half discharged for the common case. Notably the ac-3 e2e proof (e2e/tests/48) runs against a vocab-fixture OVERRIDE template whose body ('## Delivery Notes', no design-notes TODO) sidesteps this, and its assertions only check the frontmatter default placeholders ('TODO: replace with the real problem/outcome') are absent — so the default embedded-template behavior of body TODOs persisting beside filled fields is never exercised in a browser. (confidence 0.35) — Same cross-surface constraint as cli-creation's judged-todo-free-rendering-body-prose (already accepted-deviation): the form fills the frontmatter field-bound positions (the canonical source of truth), but the scaffold templates' BODY sections (## Problem/## Outcome carrying 'TODO: design notes.') are frozen byte-for-byte by spec/scaffold-templates and cannot be rewritten from this story. ac-3's literal wording ('TODO-free in every position whose field was actually filled') is technically satisfied — frontmatter is the only field-bound position, which the judge acknowledges — and fail-closed behavior is correct. The intent-gap (a reader still sees a body TODO under the Problem heading) is a cross-surface scaffold-body-TODO limitation, queued for ratification/stage-3 precision (either a shared 'field-bound positions' wording clarification across the creation-surface ACs, or a future scaffold-templates story binding body sections). Honesty slice, not a fix-class defect.

### Candidates awaiting reaffirmation

(none)

## Not resurfaced

- **judged-create-owners-value-shape** [not-resurfaced, was accepted-deviation]: Residual after the 0ad2879 fix: normalizeOwners closes the common paths (comma lists, bracketed literals, zero-name refusal by name), but an Owners value carrying a YAML-hostile character still reaches the strict-decode internal error the adjudicated finding said should never surface. An unbalanced bracket ('[alice') is not stripped and renders as the malformed literal '[[alice]'; a name containing a double quote or ': ' (e.g. 'O"Brien' or 'role: alice') normalizes to a bracketed literal that safeScalar %q-quotes into a YAML STRING, so the owners: []string position fails DecodeSpec and the caller sees 'create scaffold failed self-validation: ...' rather than the named Owners-shape refusal. Fail-closed holds (nothing lands, TestBoardSpec_Create_OwnersNormalization pins the fixed paths), but the named-refusal guarantee is complete only for well-formed names — a narrow sliver of the original issue, disclosed rather than silently considered closed. (confidence 0.20) — Disclosed residual: the named-refusal guarantee is complete for well-formed names; YAML-hostile names (unbalanced brackets, quotes, colons) fail closed with the generic self-validation error and nothing invalid ever lands. Completing the guarantee would require inventing an owner-name charset contract — a model-level question this story does not silently decide (provenance discipline); queued as a candidate model question, not a defect.
